sanskrit:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः॥
transliteration: ahaṃ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṃ pravartate | iti matvā bhajante māṃ budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: I am the source of all; from Me everything proceeds. Knowing thus, the wise, endowed with feeling, worship Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna asserts absolute ontological primacy, inviting the psyche to shift from fragmented self-referential control to surrender to a single, benevolent source. The verse reframes dependence as security rather than weakness.
psychological_state: Cognitive re-anchoring from ego-centric to source-centric locus of control; reduction of existential anxiety via radical containment.
trigger_scenario: A CEO who micromanages every department finally admits the company runs better when she trusts her empowered teams and focuses on vision, experiencing both liberation and vertigo.
anchor_text: Rest in the One from whom all things flow.
